title: Morning Light
text:
The sun rises slow and gold
over the ridge of pines,
spilling warmth across the field
where the dog waits, patient.

title: River Song
text:
Water curls around the stones,
singing its low tune.
The heron stands in silver light
and does not move till noon.

title: Empty Room
text:
A chair beside the window,
dust along the sill.
The clock has stopped at half past three.
The house is very still.

title: Winter Garden
text:
Frost has taken every leaf.
The roses bow their heads.
Beneath the snow the bulbs still dream
of April flower beds.

title: Ocean at Night
text:
The waves come in like breathing,
slow and dark and deep.
The lighthouse turns its yellow eye
across the cliffs of sleep.

title: Bread
text:
Flour on the counter,
yeast alive with heat.
My grandmother's hands still shape the loaves
the family gathers to eat.

title: The Crow
text:
He sits atop the fence post,
black against the grey.
He watches with a tilted head
and has nothing to say.

title: Old Letters
text:
I found them in a shoebox,
creased and turning brown.
Your handwriting still leans to the right
in that tiny college town.

title: Fog
text:
The city disappears in white.
The bridge is just a guess.
Footsteps pass without a face—
the world made weightless.

title: Walking Home
text:
The streetlamps flicker on at five.
Leaves blow across the park.
My breath becomes a little cloud.
I hurry through the dark.
